summary: We recommend shipping the /loop 5m orchestrator with three files committed together so any session can resume cleanly. The tradeoff is one larger PR over three small ones, but the heartbeat would have nothing to read without the queue and no operating model without the memory file. The /loop heartbeat will then re-enter every five minutes, pick the lowest-id pending outcome whose depends_on are all done, and advance it by exactly one atomic step. The orchestrator is forbidden from inventing new outcomes or stopping the loop.

claims.statement: outcomes QUEUE.md exists at outcomes-mcp outcomes QUEUE.md with 31 outcomes across tracks A through F, each row carrying status depends_on and runroc_path columns plus a tick log section for the heartbeat to append to.

citations.title: loop-prompt.md committed in this PR cites QUEUE.md as the source of truth

claims.caveats: Two outcomes B2 and the like may be subsumed into adjacent items during execution; the queue is a starting plan not a contract.

claims.statement: loop heartbeat prompt committed at claude loop-prompt.md is self-contained and re-readable each tick because it specifies the working directory the read-queue-pick-advance-update-report sequence and the rules forbidding inventing new outcomes or stopping the loop.

citations.title: loop-prompt.md is the canonical heartbeat payload
Claude Code scheduled tasks documentation describes fixed-interval /loop and self-paced modes

claims.caveats: The heartbeat assumes prior conversation history is preserved across ticks; on a fresh session it must re-read all three files first.

claims.statement: orchestrator memory file pointed at by MEMORY.md describes the operating model and the no-architecture-by-asking rule under which the orchestrator uses canonical objects claude-code-guide and the verifier MCP rather than pinging the operator for decisions.

citations.title: HeuristicVerifier reference implementation in the spec repo

claims.caveats: Memory file lives outside the repo at the operator's local Claude projects directory; this PR does not commit the memory file itself only documents that it exists.

open_questions: Should the heartbeat self-terminate when the queue reaches IDLE or should it keep ticking and report IDLE every five minutes until the operator presses Esc?
methodology_notes: Three files written in sequence with verifier-friendly structure. Bootstrap pair verified locally against the python HeuristicVerifier before pushing. The /loop syntax and stop-condition semantics were confirmed via the claude-code-guide subagent rather than asking the operator.
